commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The protected maintainer label and security-sensitive Matrix package/shrinkwrap metadata make the remaining action maintainer/secops authorization, not an automated code repair.; Needs attention: The code change removes an injectable command seam, but the external PR also changes Matrix package version and shrinkwrap metadata that require current-head maintainer/secops authorization before merge. commands.review_summary:
The PR removes Matrix crypto bootstrap command/executable injection, runs the package-local native crypto `download-lib.js` with `process.execPath`, updates focused tests, bumps Matrix plugin package/shrinkwrap/changelog metadata to `2026.6.27`, and refreshes `docs/docs_map.md`.

PR surface: Source -10, Tests -3, Docs +6, Config 0. Total -7 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main exposes injectable `runCommand` and `nodeExecutable` parameters on the Matrix native crypto repair path. I did not run a packaged Matrix install locally.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected `maintainer` label and security-sensitive Matrix package/shrinkwrap metadata make the remaining action maintainer/secops authorization, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(matrix): use fixed crypto bootstrap command
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Matrix plugin's packaged crypto bootstrap surfaced as suspicious in ClawHub because the artifact exposed an injectable Node executable / command-runner path around the native Matrix crypto dependency repair command.

## Why This Change Was Made

The runtime only needs to run the package-local `download-lib.js` with the current Node executable. This removes the test-only command and executable injection seam from production code while keeping the same fixed argv, cwd, timeout, bounded output, retry, and incomplete-binding cleanup behavior.

The plugin package version is bumped to `2026.6.27` so ClawHub can publish and scan a new immutable Matrix artifact instead of reusing the already-published `2026.6.10` tarball.

## User Impact

Matrix users keep the same automatic native crypto runtime repair behavior. The new package artifact scans clean locally under the deployed ClawHub static scanner and LLM evaluator, so installing the official Matrix plugin should no longer require users to review this bootstrap warning.

## Evidence

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/deps.test.ts
node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --package-dir extensions/matrix --check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/matrix/src/matrix/deps.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/deps.test.ts extensions/matrix/package.json extensions/matrix/npm-shrinkwrap.json
node --import tsx scripts/plugin-clawhub-release-check.ts --plugins @openclaw/matrix
node --import tsx scripts/plugin-npm-release-check.ts --plugins @openclaw/matrix
OPENCLAW_CLAWHUB_PACK_OUTPUT_DIR=/tmp/claw-434-matrix-pack-output3 bash scripts/plugin-clawhub-publish.sh --pack extensions/matrix
```

Additional local ClawHub proof against the packed `@openclaw/matrix@2026.6.27` artifact:

```text
staticScan.status: clean
staticScan.engineVersion: v2.4.26
llmAnalysis.status: clean
llmAnalysis.verdict: benign
```
target.author: patrick-erichsen
